summary: Move BSON types to top-level BSON namespace
description:
All BSON types are supposed to be in a top-level "BSON" namespace - not a child of "MongoDB".

See http://php.net/bson

summary: MongoDB\BSON\UTCDateTime::__constructor doesn't accept string argument
Segfault with iteration when not using a command cursor
description:
I tried running PHPLIB's test suite (at [this commit|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-library-prototype/commit/3e4d54b846b6cb60ab101f399ba991daface8ddf], without BSON renames) with HHVM 3.7.2 and a fresh build of the extension (at [this commit|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-hhvm-driver-prototype/commit/16263190f3f8e398dda3e46cd11ec1a84ed3911a]), and the test suite yielded a few errors and ultimately a segfault. I ran the suite with:

{noformat}
$ hhvm `which phpunit` --filter ClientFunctionalTest
{noformat}

Here's all I was able to extrapolate from the core dump:

{noformat}
#0  0x00007f748e3987bd in bson_iter_next () from /etc/hhvm/extensions/mongodb.so
(gdb) bt full
#0  0x00007f748e3987bd in bson_iter_next () from /etc/hhvm/extensions/mongodb.so
No symbol table info available.
#1  0x00007f748e38b92b in HPHP::c_MongoDBDriverCursor_ni_toArray(HPHP::ObjectData*) () from /etc/hhvm/extensions/mongodb.so
No symbol table info available.
#2  0x0000000002ac76ae in void HPHP::Native::callFunc<false, false>(HPHP::Func const*, void*, HPHP::TypedValue*, HPHP::TypedValue&) ()
No symbol table info available.
#3  0x000000000107e081 in HPHP::TypedValue* HPHP::Native::methodWrapper<false, false>(HPHP::ActRec*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#4  0x00000000086489e0 in ?? ()
No symbol table info available.
#5  0x0000000002a87646 in enterTCHelper ()
No symbol table info available.
#6  0x00000000014dfbc6 in HPHP::jit::x64::(anonymous namespace)::BackEnd::enterTCHelper(unsigned char*, HPHP::ActRec*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#7  0x000000000136453b in HPHP::jit::MCGenerator::enterTC(unsigned char*, HPHP::ActRec*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#8  0x0000000001012a96 in HPHP::enterVM(HPHP::ActRec*, HPHP::StackArgsState, HPHP::Resumable*, HPHP::ObjectData*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#9  0x0000000002a86d8a in HPHP::ExecutionContext::invokeFunc(HPHP::TypedValue*, HPHP::Func const*, HPHP::Variant const&, HPHP::ObjectData*, HPHP::Class*, HPHP::VarEnv*, HPHP::StringData*, HPHP::ExecutionContext::InvokeFlags) ()
No symbol table info available.
#10 0x0000000002ad9108 in HPHP::ExecutionContext::invokeUnit(HPHP::TypedValue*, HPHP::Unit const*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#11 0x0000000002ad8f87 in HPHP::invoke_file(HPHP::String const&, bool, char const*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#12 0x0000000000b89059 in HPHP::include_impl_invoke(HPHP::String const&, bool, char const*) ()
No symbol table info available.
#13 0x0000000000df6768 in HPHP::hphp_invoke(HPHP::ExecutionContext*, std::string const&, bool, HPHP::Array const&, HPHP::VRefParamValue const&, std::string const&, std::string const&, bool&, std::string&, bool, bool, bool) ()
No symbol table info available.

#16 0x0000000000e0df2e in HPHP::execute_program(int, char**) ()
No symbol table info available.
#17 0x0000000000b6f7f8 in main ()
No symbol table info available.
{noformat}

Oddly, if I only execute the individual test (with {{--filter testListDatabases}}), it errors instead of segfaulting. I'm not sure if this is due to the test suite executing multiple tests within the same process (although I'd argue that the driver should still handle that).

summary: Use electionId to detect stale primaries.
Implement getWriteConcern() and getReadPreference() methods on Manager
description: This is necessary for PHPLIB to obtain the write concern and read preference from parsed URI options stored on the libmongoc client object.

summary: Typo in bitwise assignment
The constructors of value objects that represent internal structures should be private
description: MongoDB/Driver/CursorID and MongoDB/Driver/Cursor ctors should be marked as private

summary: Manager->executeUpdate() option is supposed to be 'multi'
description:
Both Manager->executeUpdate() and BulkWrite->update() were using a wrong option name for 'update multiple documents'.

The correct name is 'multi'.

This affected both the docs and phongo.

summary: BsonToVariantConvertor should throw exceptions instead of warnings
description:
PHPC currently triggers warnings for the following:

 * "Could not read document from reader"
 * "Could not initialize BSON iterator"
 * "Reading document did not exhaust input buffer"

Aside from {{src/contrib/php-ssl.c}}, these are the only errors/warnings emitted by the extension. We should change these to exceptions.

Additionally, this would resolve some test output differences between PHPC and HHVM (see: [PR #66|https://github.com/10gen-labs/mongo-php-driver-prototype/pull/66]).

summary: Server::executeBulkWrite() should take a write concern
description: {{Server::executeBulkWrite()}} currently takes no write concern and inherits from the client object. Should it take an optional write concern like its Manager counterpart? In PHPLIB, we need to select the primary manually for APIs that depend on wire version checking, and I'm thinking of a case where legacy index creation would want to ensure we only use w:1 on the insertion.

summary: BSON encoding should ignore protected/private properties
Make Manager::getWriteConcern() and getReadPreference() return value objects
description: Currently, these methods return arrays in the form of each value object's debug output. We should instead return the actual value objects, so the return values may be used with methods expecting a WriteConcern or ReadPreference.

summary: Prevent MongoDB\BSON\Type objects from serializing as root element
Implement getters for ReadPreference and WriteConcern properties
description: Getters may be useful so that users can inspect these objects without resorting to the debug handlers.

summary: WriteConcern constructor should throw for invalid $w and $wtimeout values
description:
{{mongoc_write_concern_set_w()}} requires the argument to be >= -3 or it is a NOP. We should raise an exception if {{$w}} is an integer < -3.

Additionally, should we raise an exception if the {{$wtimeout}} argument is < 0. Currently, libmongoc doesn't check for this and allows a negative timeout to be set, which may be an oversight. Per the [documentation|http://docs.mongodb.org/manual/reference/write-concern/#wtimeout], {{wtimeout}} should be >= 0.

description:
{noformat}

summary: Default DSN is not parsable
description: The default is "localhost", but should be "mongodb://localhost"

summary: BulkWrite and executeInsert() should support an options array
description:
{{executeUpdate()}} and {{executeDelete()}} currently take an options array (between the query arguments and WriteConcern); however, {{executeBulkWrite()}} and {{executeInsert()}} do not and BulkWrite's constructor only takes a boolean {{$ordered}} argument.

Current protos:

 * public function Manager::executeBulkWrite(string $namespace, BulkWrite $bulk, ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function Manager::executeInsert(string $namespace, mixed $document, ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function BulkWrite::__construct(boolean $ordered)

This leaves no way to support a "bypassDocumentValidation" option at the basic driver level. We will need to change these prototypes to allow passing of such an option (and perhaps others down the line). Since we can create a BulkWrite with options, there is no need to change {{executeBulkWrite()}}.

Proposed protos:

 * public function executeBulkWrite(string $namespace, BulkWrite $bulk, ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function executeInsert(string $namespace, mixed $document, ?array $insertOptions = [], ?WriteConcern $writeConcern = null): WriteResult
 * public function BulkWrite::__construct(?array $bulkWriteOptions = [])

Actually implementation of "bypassDocumentValidation" will be handled by HHVM-116. This issue only pertains to modifying our public API to support an options array, to which we'll later add bypassDocumentValidation".

summary: toJSON and fromJSON should throw exceptions on errors
description:
{{toJSON()}} should throw UnexpectedValueExceptions (just like {{toPHP()}}):

 * Could not read document from BSON reader
 * Reading document did not exhaust input buffer

{{fromJSON()}} should throw an UnexpectedValueException on any error parsing the JSON. For now, it's suitable to simply report the {{bson_error_t}} message.

summary: Support bypassDocumentValidation option for insert and update commands
description: We need to be able to specify a "bypassDocumentValidation" option for insert and update operations. This means we need to modify BulkWrite, {{executeInsert()}}, and {{executeUpdate()}}. HHVM-117 will be required before we can support options on BulkWrite and {{executeInsert()}}.

summary: Link in OpenSSL/SASL and don't rely on other bits to do that
Upgrade bundled libbson and libmongoc to 1.3.1
Make it work with HHVM 3.12
HHVM 3.11 adds additional data types that we need to support
The Ghost bug
Driver does not pick on up default writeConcern
Query debug handler should defer to ReadConcern handler for readConcern option
description: Presently, the readConcern option is stored as a string inside the query. Output should be consistent with PHPC-539.

summary: Parse readconcernlevel in URI options array
description:
According to {{mongoc_uri_parse_option()}} in {{mongoc-uri.c}}, "readconcernlevel" is only handled when processing the URI's query string. This means that PHPC may not be properly assigning the value when picking it up from Manager's {{$options}} array argument (instead of the URI string).

This should be trivial to test, given that we now have a {{Manager::getReadConcern()}} method.

summary: Make sure we bundle all source code in release tarballs
description: See also https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/issues/44

summary: Bug with BulkWrite->update() and choosing between replace() or update()
Manager::selectServer() should select exception class based on bson_error_t
description: Currently, {{Manager::selectServer()}} always throws a RuntimeException, which is inconsistent with the other three execute methods. Combined, these are the four methods that could result in connection initialization, so they should handle exceptions in a like manner.

summary: Only set readConcern when it's 'local' or 'majority'
description: Currently we don't check whether the readConcern is not NULL when setting it on the C driver's query structure, resulting in an RunTimeException when executing. This breaks the PHP Library tests quite massively.

summary: Autoloading is not triggered for user defined classes
WriteResult should always include the effective write concern
description:
Two PHPC tests failed on HHVM because the WriteResult was not set with the applied WriteConcern (either the default or inherited from some URI options):

 * MongoDB\Driver\WriteResult::isAcknowledged() with default WriteConcern [phpc/tests/standalone/writeresult-isacknowledged-001.phpt]
 * MongoDB\Driver\WriteResult::isAcknowledged() with inherited WriteConcern [phpc/tests/standalone/writeresult-isacknowledged-002.phpt]

summary: Throw exception if Binary subtype is out of range
description:
The [BSON specification|http://bsonspec.org/spec.html] defines a binary subtype as one byte. Since PHP can only parse it as an integer, we should throw an exception if the argument is out of range (i.e. less than zero or greater than 255).

Currently, a larger integer will be silently truncated when assigned to an {{uint8_t}} in {{bson_append_binary()}}.

summary: Throw exception if WriteConcern wtimeout is out of range
description:
libmongoc limits wtimeout to a signed, 32-bit integer.

We already throw an exception if wtimeout is negative, but we should also throw if the value is > INT32_MAX; otherwise, input might be silently truncated.

summary: Throw exception if Timestamp arguments are out of range
description: The [BSON specification|http://bsonspec.org/spec.html] defines a timestamp's components as two 32-bit integer values (timestamp and increment). If a 64-bit integer is passed on an allowing platform, the values could be silently truncated to {{uint32_t}} types when calling {{bson_append_timestamp()}}. We should throw an exception if the arguments exceed the range of 32-bit integers.

summary: Use more descriptive messages in WriteExceptions
toJSON() should throw on invalid BSON
description:
Currently, {{MongoDB\BSON\toJSON()}} only throws if we cannot read exactly one document from the input stream.

Corrupt BSON (e.g. flipping a field's type  to the wrong byte) causes {{bson_as_json()}} to return NULL, which we simply return as an empty string. Instead, we should check for NULL and raise an exception.

summary: ObjectID only supports lower case hexadecimal letters
description: See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/253

summary: Throw exception for failed root or nested BSON iteration
description:
When document and array visitors encounter corrupt BSON, they should signal to their calling context to stop further iteration. If {{bson_iter_visit_all()}} fails (due to aborted iteration and/or an error offset), an UnexpectedValueException should be thrown with the message: "Could not convert BSON document to a PHP variable".

Currently, BsonToVariantConverter leaves the PHP value unpopulated (e.g. empty stdClass instance) and may echo "converting corrupt" to stdout for some, but not all, cases.

summary: Driver fails to build due to "Logger" with HHVM 3.9-3.11
Rephrase unsupported/corrupt BSON messages
Free server descriptions with mongoc_server_descriptions_destroy_all()
description: See [this GitHub comment|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/290#discussion_r59271875] for PHPC-600's PR.

summary: Improve connection handling
description: Currently connections are not shared between requests. We should ideally also allow the SDAM state to be persisted, but that depends on functionality in the CDRIVER.

summary: Report bypassDocumentValidation in BulkWrite debug output
Do not use mongoc_cursor_t private API in Cursor debug handler
description:
Per discussion in PHPC-688, the debug handler should report the following:

 * database
 ** Combined with "collection", this covers the old "ns" field.
 * collection
 ** Combined with "database", this covers the old "ns" field. The value will be null for commands (previously, "$cmd" would have been present in the old "ns" field).
 * query
 ** If the Cursor was created via {{executeQuery()}}, this will be the corresponding MongoDB\Driver\Query object. This covers the following old fields: query, fields, flags, skip, limit, batch_size.
 * command
 ** If the Cursor was created via {{executeCommand()}}, this will be the corresponding MongoDB\Driver\Command object. This covers the old "is_command" field, which previously would have been false for a command cursor.
 * readPreference
 ** The corresponding MongoDB\Driver\ReadPreference object passed to the execute method that created this Cursor. This will be null if the argument was unused, which means that the read preference of the underlying client (i.e. MongoDB\Driver\Manager) was used instead. The old "read_preference" field was always populated (either from the execute method argument or client).
 * isDead
 ** This identical to the {{isDead()}} method. It does not directly cover the old fields: sent, done, end_of_event, in_exhaust.
 * server
 ** The MongoDB\Driver\Server object corresponding to the sever on which the Cursor was executed. This replaces the old "server_id" integer field and may be more helpful for debugging.
 * currentIndex
 ** This replaces the old "count" field and reports the iterator's current key. It defaults to zero.
 * currentDocument
 ** This is unchanged. The value is the iterator's current element (if any). It may be null.

summary: Do not allow BulkWrite objects to be executed multiple times
description: Although libmongoc allows a {{mongoc_bulk_operation_t}} to be executed multiple times, it does not make sense to do so. It is also prone to duplicate key errors, since we generate {{_id}} values when documents are scheduled for insertion (see: PHPC-360).

summary: Driver does not compile with HHVM 3.14
description: HHVM had removed some APIs.

summary: Rename "javascript" to "code" in Javascript BSON class
description: The BSON spec and forthcoming getter method (HHVM-233) refer to "code", so we should use that term for the internal property as well. This has implications for {{var_dump()}}, {{var_export()}}, and serialization.

summary: Javascript constructor should throw if code contains null bytes
description: Although http://bsonspec.org/spec.html might seem to allow this, libbson does not permit null bytes in Javascript code strings.

summary: Default Manager URI to "mongodb://127.0.0.1/"
description:
PHPC has defaulted to this value (via libmongoc) since 1.1.1 but will now explicitly set that default URI (see: PHPC-746). HHVM currently defaults to "mongodb://localhost", which might prefer IPv6 connections.

For consistency, let's change the default URI to "mongodb://127.0.0.1/".

summary: Improve error messages for invalid Decimal128 and ObjectID strings
description:
As mentioned in [PR #346|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/pull/346], PHPC and HHVM differ in their Decimal128 exception message for an invalid string. For the ObjectID message, both drivers are consistent, but the error message is brief and does not report the invalid value.

I propose we change these messages to the following:

 * Error parsing Decimal128 string: %s
 * Error parsing ObjectID string: %s

HHVM currently refers to "The argument", which I'd like to remove, since this error will be common to {{\_\_construct()}}, {{\_\_set_state()}}, and {{\_\_wakeup()}}. It is merely a field instead of a proper method argument for the last two functions.

summary: Regex constructor should default flags arg to empty string
description:
This seems like a usability improvement (similar to HHVM-162 for UTCDateTime).

The flags field can be left as required for {{\_\_wakeup()}} and {{\_\_set_state()}}, as it should always be present in {{serialize()}} and {{var_export()}} output.

summary: UTCDateTime should support microseconds
description: HHVM makes it hard to set the fraction on returned DateTime objects, as there is no direct access to the "f" property. Add a workaround to make this work.

summary: Validate that read preference tags is an array of documents
description:
We should validate that a read preference tag set is an array of documents when applying URI options and constructing a ReadPreference object.

As mentioned in [mongodb/mongo-php-driver#384|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/issues/384], libmongoc may segfault when encountering unexpected BSON for the tag set.

summary: BulkWrite::update() silently ignores invalid arguments
Regex constructor should throw if pattern or flags contain null bytes
description: BSON's regex type uses null-terminated strings for pattern and flag components. Since values would be truncated by libbson, which uses {{strlen()}} internally, we should throw if either argument contains a null byte.

summary: Ensure read preference tags sets serialize as documents
description: An empty tag set in the Manager options or ReadPreference constructor currently serializes as an empty PHP array. We should ensure that this is serialized as an empty BSON document (not an array) in the read preference structure.

summary: Unacknowledged WriteResults have null counts
description:
Starting with 1.4.0-dev, libmongoc now omits the count fields from the write result BSON for unacknowledged writes. This causes our methods to return {{null}}, while they previously returned zero.

I propose we allow this behavior change and document it as such in PHPC-653. {{getModifiedCount}} already had behavior to conditionally return null, since it is omitted from acknowledged writes to older servers. This would extend that behavior of "null if unavailable" to the other count fields.

Note: The getter methods in PHPLIB already throw for unacknowledged writes (PHPLIB-68).

summary: Query execution should not assign read concern to client
description:
See: https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/commit/6276c1b2f2cc541ab00dd9d3713867f48200bd93#diff-276bb9c557784a4aa37a896b7bae213eR364

The read concern should be assigned to the temporary collection. As-is, it's possible for the read concern to linger after the query is executed and apply to future operations.

summary: WriteResult debug handler should return objects
description: Currently, WriteResult's debug handler flattens its internal objects (e.g. upserted IDs, write errors) to arrays.

summary: Fix compile issue with HHVM 3.15
Drivers must raise an error if response messageLength > ismaster.maxMessageSizeBytes
Empty ReadConcern and WriteConcern should serialize to BSON documents
description:
Since ReadConcern and WriteConcern may be empty by default, it's possible for their {{bsonSerialize()}} methods to return an empty array. This will result in serialization to a BSON array, which would be rejected by the server. We should ensure an object is always returned from these methods.

This is not an issue for ReadPreference, since it will always contain at least a "mode" field; however, we can also return an object for good measure.

summary: BSON objects should implement JsonSerializable
UTCDateTime::jsonSerialize() should return a $numberLong field
Javascript::jsonSerialize() should return a document with $code and $scope fields
fromJSON() should not evaluate bson_error_t.message as boolean
description:
In {{bson.c}}, {{fromJson}} calls {{phongo_throw_exception}} with either a custom error message, or a generic "Error parsing JSON" message.

To determine whether or not to use a custom error message (bubbled up from mongoc) it checks whether a {{bson_error_t}}'s {{message}} field is NULL. Since the {{message}} field is just an array, it is never NULL. This produces a warning on some compilers.

To check if the {{bson_error_t}} has an error message, {{error.code}} or {{error.domain}} should be checked instead.

summary: Mark \MongoDB\Driver\Manager as final
Back out APM from 1.2
ObjectIDs don't compare properly
description: See https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/issues/156 as well as HHVM failing to pass PHPC test "tests/bson/bson-objectid-001.phpt".

summary: Javascript object serialization in libmongoc changed
description:
This bug dates back to the very first release and was introduced in [dfbef38|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-php-driver/commit/dfbef380a579e2d0eba236c0faa53e559d380578] for PHPC-74.

Javascript objects with a scope are erroneously serialized as BSON code types without a scope. Javascript objects without a scope are erroneously serialized as BSON code_with_scope types; however, since their scope pointer is null, {{bson_append_code_with_scope()}} defensively falls back to {{bson_append_code()}}.

This was discovered while updating the PHPC-459 tests for libmongoc-1.5.0-rc6, which includes CDRIVER-1335.

summary: Assertion failure in stream_not_found() after failed getmore operation
description:
PHPC has two tests for throwing an exception on a failed getmore. These tests spin up their own server with mongo-orchestration and configure the failReceivedGetmore fail point.

After upgrading from 1.5.0-rc3 to 1.5.0-rc4, both of these tests trigger an assert failure in libmongoc:

{noformat}
phpc/src/libmongoc/src/mongoc/mongoc-cluster.c:1470 stream_not_found(): precondition failed: error
Aborted (core dumped)

Termsig=6
{noformat}

Based on the libmongoc trace log, this assert failure happens when the {{mongoc_client_t}} is destroyed during process shutdown. Our trace logs end with:

{noformat}
OK: Got MongoDB\Driver\Exception\ConnectionException
===DONE===
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     cursor: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_cursor_destroy():493
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00] cursor-cursorid: TRACE   > ENTRY: _mongoc_cursor_cursorid_destroy():53
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     cursor: TRACE   > ENTRY: _mongoc_cursor_destroy():510
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     client: TRACE   > ENTRY: _mongoc_client_kill_cursor():1608
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_cluster_stream_for_server():1541
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   > ENTRY: _mongoc_cluster_stream_for_server_description():1475
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_cluster_disconnect_node():1305
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   >  EXIT: mongoc_cluster_disconnect_node():1317
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     mongoc: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_server_description_handle_ismaster():472
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     mongoc: TRACE   >  EXIT: mongoc_server_description_handle_ismaster():478
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     mongoc: TRACE   > TRACE: mongoc_topology_description_handle_ismaster():1674 No transition entry to Single for Unknown
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   >  EXIT: _mongoc_cluster_stream_for_server_description():1507
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_cluster_disconnect_node():1305
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   >  EXIT: mongoc_cluster_disconnect_node():1317
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     mongoc: TRACE   > ENTRY: mongoc_server_description_destroy():151
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     mongoc: TRACE   >  EXIT: mongoc_server_description_destroy():157
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]    cluster: TRACE   >  EXIT: mongoc_cluster_stream_for_server():1563
[2016-11-16T17:03:21+00:00]     cursor: TRACE   >  EXIT: _mongoc_cursor_destroy():549

summary: Fix compile issue with HHVM 3.17
description: HHVM 3.17 has broken internal APIs (again). This needs to be addressed.

summary: Alphabetize Regex flags when instantiating from BSON
description: PHPC-829 addressed alphabetizing Regex flags when constructing a new object or instantiating from a serialized value, but also need to alphabetize flags when instantiating from raw BSON data.

summary: readConcern option should not be included in getMore commands
description: See CDRIVER-2003 for more details. This is a tracking ticket for bumping our libmongoc submodule once the issue is resolved upstream.

summary: Remove "#undef TRACE" clutch
description:
Revert https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/pull/123/commits/ae057f5a125d35b82762436a32ccbcf92b235c8d once CDRIVER and HHVM have fixed it:

- https://jira.mongodb.org/browse/CDRIVER-1486
- https://github.com/facebook/hhvm/issues/7288

summary: ReadPreference constructor should accept strings
description: The ReadPreference constructor should accept string values consistent with the [readPreference URI option|https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/#urioption.readPreference]. To preserve BC, we should still accept the integer constants.

summary: Driver should validate BSON documents before insert and update
MONGOC_ERROR_COMMAND_INVALID_ARG should yield InvalidArgumentException
description:
Currently, {{MONGOC_ERROR_COMMAND_INVALID_ARG}} error codes yield RuntimeExceptions. Since libmongoc uses this code for argument validation errors in its own API  (before commands would be sent to the server), InvalidArgumentException would be more fitting. Typically, RuntimeExceptions indicate errors originating from the server.

This is relevant to PHPC-712 and CDRIVER-2018.

summary: Keep mongo_ssl_opt_t.pem_file valid for life of mongoc_client_t
Add MongoDB\BSON\toExtendedJSON() to wrap bson_as_extended_json()
description:
CDRIVER-1947 added {{bson_as_extended_json()}}, which supersedes {{bson_as_json()}}. This change will require changes to our test suite, as we currently use {{toJSON()}} for debugging and extended JSON will wrap more primitive types (e.g. integers). Alternatively, we may decide to create a separate {{toExtendedJSON()}} function in 1.3.0 and leave {{toJSON()}} as is.

Note: {{bson_as_json()}} was briefly deprecated in libbson's 1.7.0-dev branch, but that was reverted in CDRIVER-2077.

summary: Using a projection with an empty field name causes a crash when destroying cursor
description: Tracking ticket for CDRIVER-2072

summary: API breaks in HHVM 3.18 and 3.19
description: From GitHub's https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-hhvm-driver/issues/181
